_note: Hand-curated <title>/<meta description> overrides for CTR harvesting on page-1 pages. Keys: codePages by errorCode.id, errorPages by normalized disambig slug. Absent key = template default. Keep titles ≤60 chars, accurate to error-codes.json meanings. Log every change in docs/experiment-log.md.

ge-washer-e64.title: GE Washing Machine E64 Error: Failed Heater — DIY Fix
ge-washer-e64.description: E64 means the heating element has an open circuit. How to test the heater, the replacement part, and repair cost for GE washers.

samsung-washer-be2.title: Samsung Washer bE2: Stuck Button Error — Easy Fix
samsung-washer-be2.description: bE2 means a control-panel button is shorted or stuck. The 5-minute panel check, reset steps, and when the board needs replacing.

maytag-washer-f26.title: Maytag Washer F26: Door Switch Error — How to Fix
maytag-washer-f26.description: F26 means the washer can't detect the door state. Door-switch test, reset steps, part number guidance, and repair cost.

lg-washer-f6.title: LG Washer F6 Error: Motor Overcurrent — Causes & Fix
lg-washer-f6.description: F6 means excess current in the motor drive circuit. Step-by-step checks from reset to motor/board, with DIY vs pro repair costs.

miele-oven-f33.title: Miele Oven F33 Fault: Control Shutdown — What to Do
miele-oven-f33.description: F33 is an electronic-control safety shutdown. What triggers it, the reset that clears it, and when to call Miele service.

maytag-dryer-f22.title: Maytag Dryer F22: Thermistor Failure — DIY Fix
maytag-dryer-f22.description: F22 means the outlet temperature sensor failed. How to test the thermistor, the replacement part, and repair cost for Maytag dryers.

electrolux-washer-e51.title: Electrolux Washer E51: Motor Triac Fault — Fix Guide
electrolux-washer-e51.description: E51 means a motor-triac short on the control board. Diagnostic steps, board repair vs replacement, and expected costs.

samsung-refrigerator-85e.title: Samsung Fridge 85E: Low Voltage Error — What to Do
samsung-refrigerator-85e.description: 85E means the compressor supply voltage is too low. Power checks to run first, when it self-clears, and when to call a pro.

e11.title: E11 Error Code: Frigidaire & Electrolux — Meaning & Fix
e11.description: E11 on a washer means a fill problem; on dryers and ovens it differs. Pick your brand and appliance for the exact E11 meaning and fix.

e41.title: E41 Washer Error: Door Not Closed — Simple Fix
e41.description: E41 on Frigidaire and Electrolux washers means the door isn't detected as shut. The latch checks and fix, brand by brand.

cf.title: CF Error on Refrigerators: What It Means & the Fix
cf.description: CF on Whirlpool means board communication failure; on Kenmore a condenser fan fault. Pick your fridge for the exact fix.

e01.title: E01 Error Code: Bosch, Electrolux & Broan — Fixes
e01.description: E01 differs by appliance: dishwasher heating, dryer door lock, range-hood fan overload. Pick your appliance for the exact E01 fix.

f9.title: F9 Error Code: Dryer & Oven Meanings — Fix by Brand
f9.description: F9 on LG dryers is a thermistor failure; on LG, Whirlpool and GE ovens a control or door-latch fault. Find your F9 fix by brand.

6e.title: 6E Refrigerator Error: Sensor Fault — Samsung & GE Fix
6e.description: 6E on Samsung and GE refrigerators means an ambient/humidity sensor failure. The sensor test, part, and repair cost for each brand.
